title: Website Strategy Intake
description: Reference schema for the external client-facing intake form. Completed client answers belong in docs/client_intake_form.json and should preserve these field IDs.

submissionContract.responseKeyRule: Each answer should be stored under the matching question `id`.
submissionContract.emptyValueRule: Optional unanswered fields may be null, false, an empty string, or an empty array.
notes: This schema is reference material for the framework and should live outside the project docs answer data.
The actual completed intake response is the source of truth for each client project.
Redesign-only questions may be left blank for greenfield projects.

sections.title: Project Basics
sections.description: Core identity, project type, and who is involved in the project.

questions.label: Is this a brand-new website or a redesign of an existing one?
What is the business name that should appear on the website?
If the legal company name is different, what is it?
Who is the main point of contact for this project?
What is the best email for project communication?
What is the best phone number for project communication, if needed?
Do you have a preferred launch window or deadline?

sections.title: Business Profile
sections.description: What the business does, who it serves, and why it is different.

questions.label: In one or two sentences, what does the business do?
Do you already have a tagline, slogan, or core promise you want us to preserve or improve?
How did the business start, and what should people understand about its story?
What are your primary services, products, or offers?
Which services, products, or offers should the website prioritize most?
Who are your ideal customers or audience segments?
What problems, frustrations, or goals bring people to you?
What objections or hesitations do prospects usually have before they buy or contact you?
What makes your business different from competitors?
What trust signals should the website highlight? Examples: years in business, certifications, awards, reviews, guarantees, licensing, partners, or notable clients.
Are there any claims, offers, or topics we should avoid or be careful about?
What is the main business address?
Which cities, neighborhoods, regions, or states do you serve?
Which service areas matter most for lead generation or SEO?
Are there any local market conditions, seasonal factors, or regional details we should understand?
Should the business address appear publicly on the website?
Should the website include a map or location embed?

sections.title: Goals And Conversion
sections.description: Business goals, lead capture priorities, and public contact details.

questions.label: What is the main business outcome this website should support?
Are there any secondary goals the website should support?
What actions should visitors take most often on the site?
Where should leads go? Include email recipients, phone numbers, CRM, scheduling links, or routing notes.
What email should receive form leads or quote requests?
What phone number should receive call leads or urgent lead follow-up?
Which contact methods should be shown publicly on the site?
Which phone numbers should appear publicly on the website?
Should a phone number be shown publicly on the website?
Which email addresses should appear publicly on the website?
Should an email address be shown publicly on the website?
What kinds of forms does the website need? Examples: contact, quote request, consultation request, newsletter, intake, support.
Are there any lead-quality filters, required questions, compliance notes, or routing constraints we should know about?

sections.title: Brand And Design Direction
sections.description: How the site should feel, look, and avoid looking.

questions.label: How should the brand feel to visitors? Examples: high-trust, premium, friendly, bold, calm, modern, playful.
How should the website sound? Examples: direct, warm, authoritative, conversational, polished, technical.
What is the primary brand color or main color direction?
What is the secondary or accent brand color or direction?
If you know it, what is the primary brand color hex code?
If you know it, what is the secondary brand color hex code?
Describe any color preferences, palette direction, or colors to avoid if exact brand colors are not locked yet.
Do you have a logo, fonts, photos, or brand guidelines we should use?
Share websites whose design, layout, or feel you like. Include URLs.
Are there any design styles, colors, layouts, or competitor looks you want us to avoid?
What kind of imagery should the site use? Examples: real team photos, lifestyle photography, product closeups, illustrations, minimal placeholders.

sections.title: Content And Information Architecture
sections.description: What the site needs to say, what pages it needs, and what material already exists.

questions.label: What pages or sections do you know the site must include?
What should the homepage communicate first and most clearly?
What questions do customers ask most often that the website should answer?
What existing materials do you already have? Examples: brochures, old website copy, service descriptions, reviews, case studies, videos, forms, PDFs.
Are there any legal notices, disclaimers, licensing statements, or required copy blocks that must appear on the site?
Who needs to review or approve strategy, design, copy, or launch decisions?

sections.title: SEO And Discovery
sections.description: Search intent, local relevance, and discoverability priorities.

questions.label: What are the most important search terms or services you want the website to rank for?
What supporting keywords, modifiers, or related searches also matter?
Who are your main competitors or comparison sites online?
What matters most for SEO right now? Examples: local rankings, service pages, content depth, page speed, map visibility, topical authority.
What facts, credentials, stats, case studies, or evidence can we use to build trust and authority?

sections.title: Redesign Only
sections.description: Leave these blank for brand-new websites.

questions.label: What is the current website URL?
Are there pages, URLs, or sections that absolutely must be preserved?
Is there any existing copy, messaging, or SEO content that must not be lost?
What is not working on the current site today?
What existing assets or access do you already have? Examples: CMS access, analytics, search console, image library, sitemap export, copy docs.
